mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for equity valuations, directly compressing forward S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp acceleration in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity, driving negative equity returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in more than 55% of triggered instances across walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in real personal income and stable inflation indicates robust consumer purchasing power without monetary policy tightening pressure, generating positive quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: Interaction term loses statistical significance with p-value greater than 0.10 in out-of-sample regression models.

mechanism_narrative: An expanding industrial production index reflects strengthening corporate output and capacity utilization, which translates to positive short-term equity momentum during recovery phases.
failure_condition: Signal direction reverses to negative during recovery regimes with greater than 60% frequency in backtests.

mechanism_narrative: Higher quarterly real GDP growth directly correlates with improved corporate earnings trajectories, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns show no significant positive correlation with GDPC1 growth rates over rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating monthly inflation triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, which compresses equity valuations and yields negative forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive in more than 65% of high-inflation trigger months during walk-forward evaluation.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a declining unemployment rate confirms genuine labor market tightening and wage growth, supporting positive equity returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Joint signal fails to outperform single-proxy baselines by more than 10% in Sharpe ratio over walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in housing starts signals weakening residential investment and broader credit contraction, dragging down equity returns over the next quarter during downturn phases.
failure_condition: Housing starts decline fails to predict negative quarterly returns with accuracy greater than 50% during contraction regimes.
